primary_responsibility: Runs tests using public tools, URLs, or app UI features.
can_do: Submit raw captures, timestamps, requested/returned symbol lists, and observed output.
should_not_do: Claim implementation details of a third-party app without evidence.
later_app_developer_fit: App developer can also contribute as an observer if they submit comparable captures.

primary_responsibility: Maintains the master field database and schema notes.
can_do: Add field rows, update applicability, add validation rules, and mark evidence status.
should_not_do: Remove or rename stable IDs without migration notes.
later_app_developer_fit: Can help identify fields important for app exports.

primary_responsibility: Reviews whether apparent changes are real schema drift or capture anomalies.
can_do: Request retests, compare endpoints, and check type/path consistency.
should_not_do: Promote one-off nulls to permanent field removals without evidence.
later_app_developer_fit: Can provide implementation-neutral review of app-compatible outputs.

primary_responsibility: Keeps README, workflow notes, and user-facing guidance clear.
can_do: Rewrite docs, add examples, clarify non-scope, and improve public usability.
should_not_do: Turn the repository into vendor-specific support documentation.
later_app_developer_fit: Can help explain app-related observations without making the app central.

can_do: Build ZIPs, update manifests, run validators, and write changelog entries.
should_not_do: Publish releases with unclear evidence status.
later_app_developer_fit: Can coordinate app-related contributions as one source among others.

primary_responsibility: Optional future participant who may provide technical insight from a specific app.
can_do: Clarify what the app accepts, how it exports results, and how users can test safely.
should_not_do: Redirect project scope into private app support or proprietary debugging.
later_app_developer_fit: A valued contributor, not the sole owner or purpose of the project.
